chunk_summary: This section outlines the minimum viable dataset and actionable alert rules for tele-nutrition registries, emphasizing risk prioritization, clinical identifiers, and escalation protocols to ensure effective follow-up and intervention.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of a minimum viable registry dataset for tele-nutrition services.
Learn how to define actionable alert rules tied to clinical thresholds and risk escalation.
Explore methods for prioritizing risk and time in tele-nutrition dashboards.
Identify key audit and follow-up fields for maintaining registry integrity.
key_facts: Core identifiers include unique child ID, name, age, sex, caregiver details, and preferred language.
Clinical fields include anthropometric data (weight, height/length, MUAC) and derived indices (WAZ, HAZ, WHZ, BAZ).
Alert rules include MUAC <115 mm for ages 6–59 months and hemoglobin <7 g/dL triggering urgent referral.
Measurement confidence is categorized as high (clinic/device verified), moderate (coached home readings), or low (uncertain).
Escalation fields include risk tier, red flag presence, and escalation completion date/facility.
Follow-up fields track next due date, overdue status, missed contact count, and assigned team member.
reference_quote: A registry is clinically useful only if it drives the next action: recall, referral, escalation, or deescalation.
document_key_topics: Minimum viable registry dataset for tele-nutrition
Actionable alert rules for clinical thresholds
Follow-up and audit protocols in registries
